mappings.rationale: Covers macroeconomic concepts including national income, aggregate output, and employment theory.
Directly covers Keynesian theory of effective demand, unemployment, and countercyclical fiscal policy (subsections A.1 and C.3).
Covers business cycles, economic fluctuations, and the dynamics of capitalist macroeconomies.

mappings.vsiAuthor: John O. S. Wilson, John Goddard
mappings.rationale: Covers monetary institutions, exchange rates, and international monetary cooperation relevant to subsections B.1 and B.2.
Covers international economic integration, foreign exchange markets, and balance of payments issues.

mappings.vsiTitle: The Great Depression and The New Deal
mappings.rationale: Covers a major business cycle event and countercyclical policy responses (subsections C.1 and C.3).
Covers international economic institutions and trade arrangements relevant to international equilibrium.
Covers monetary and fiscal policy approaches to macroeconomic management.
Covers historical business cycles, economic crises, and international financial developments.
Covers financial mathematics used in analyzing exchange rates, interest rates, and monetary policy.
Covers the classical law of markets and theories foundational to macroeconomic thought (subsection A.1.a).
Covers macroeconomic dynamics of major emerging economies and their role in international financial equilibrium.
Provides a case study of macroeconomic management including fiscal policy, exchange rates, and international trade.
Covers Marxist theories of business cycles and economic crises as inherent features of capitalism.
Covers macroeconomic challenges of developing countries including balance of payments and exchange rate management.
